principle: Always provide three ways to close a modal: an X button, a Cancel button, and clicking the overlay outside
explanation: X.ux|modal_escape: user_must_have_control; 3_exit_routes: X_button_top_right + Cancel_CTA + click_overlay_outside; all_three_required; modal_without_escape == trap -> frustration; NO:modal_closeable_only_by_completing_action NO:no_X_button NO:overlay_click_does_nothing eg: "modal_only_closeable_via_submit" >> "modal: X_top_right + Cancel_btn + overlay_click_dismisses"

principle: Reserve modals for irreversible or high-stakes actions only — use inline messages for regular errors
explanation: X.ux|modal_when: regular_errors -> inline_message NOT modal; modal_reserved_for: delete_account + remove_data + major_purchase + irreversible_actions; overusing_modals -> annoyed_users + broken_flow; cost_of_mistake_is_high -> modal adds_safety_layer; NO:modal_for_every_form_error NO:modal_for_low_stakes_confirmations eg: "modal: Are you sure you want to log out?" >> "inline_toast: logged_out (no_modal_needed)"

principle: Frame modal confirmation questions positively and avoid double negatives that force users to reason through No to a negative question
explanation: X.ux|modal_copy: double_negative: "sure_you_don't_want_to_keep?" + "No" == confusing; fix: positive_frame + tied_to_action; question == "Are_you_sure_you_want_to_[action]?"; buttons: Cancel+[Action_verb] NOT Yes+No; NO:"don't_you_want_to_keep_your_account?" NO:"Yes"+"No"_for_destructive eg: "Don't keep? No/Yes" >> "Deactivate account? Cancel | Deactivate"

principle: Add a step progress indicator inside multi-step modals so users always know where they are in the process
explanation: X.ux|modal_multistep: complex_task_in_modal -> step_indicator_at_top (Upload_CSV -> Check_data -> Confirm); user_sees_position + steps_remaining; keeps_context_of_current_page; NO:multi_step_modal_without_progress NO:navigating_away_from_page_for_complex_tasks eg: "modal_no_steps_user_lost" >> "modal: [1_Upload]→[2_Check]→[3_Confirm] + current_step_highlighted"
